title: Realtime presence has stopped working
description:
# Bug report

Realtime presence events (sync, join, leave) stopped firing  on 05/03/2026 (or maybe 6th). This occurred without any changes to our application code.

Interestingly, the issue does not persist locally (using Supabase CLI/Docker), but it is consistent across our production and staging environments.

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug

- The browser Network tab shows incoming WebSocket messages.
- The Supabase Dashboard logs show presence events being tracked.
- The high-level SDK listeners (e.g., .on('presence', { event: 'sync' }, ... )) simply never trigger.
- We can "force" it to work by listening to the internal/undocumented presence_state and presence_diff events directly, though this causes TypeScript errors.

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository:

1. Implement realtime as described in the docs
2. Try to run it with a deployed supabase instance

## Expected behavior

Realtime presence should work according to the docs.

## Versions:
We initially observed the issue with version `"@supabase/supabase-js": "2.48.4"` but we also had the issue with the latest version `2.98.0`

## Additional context

We are currently forced to use this implementation to keep the app running (ignoring any type errors), which suggests the underlying data is fine, but the SDK's event-matching logic is failing:

```ts
   const channelRoom = supabase.channel(channel, {
                config: { presence: { key: "", enabled: true } },
            })
            channelRoom

                .on("presence_state", {}, (event) => {
                    // Initial state
                    const items = Object.values(event)
                    const userIds = items.map((item) => item.metas[0]?.user)
                    userIds.forEach((userId) => dispatch(addUser({ channel, user: userId })))
                })
                .on("presence_diff", {}, (event) => {
                    const joinedUserIds = Object.values(event.joins).map((item) => item.metas[0]?.user)
                    joinedUserIds.forEach((id) => dispatch(addUser({ channel, user: id })))

                    const leftUserIds = Object.values(event.leaves).map((item) => item.metas[0]?.user)
                    leftUserIds.forEach((id) => dispatch(removeUser({ channel, user: id })))
                })
                .subscribe()

            setRoom(channelRoom)
        }
        subscribe()

```

title: Dashboard row insert doesn't work properly due to dashboard insert logic not differentiate between "doing nothing" or inserting NULL
description:
# Bug report


- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug
First bug is how I found out about this. Second one is the bug described in title, which is cause of all.

First.
Row inserting while impersonating fails for bool type columns when column has no privilege to insert for given role. Becuase supabase dashboard tries to insert bool without checking whether its column default.

Second.
Inserting NULL from dashboard doesnt work because dashboard thinks user wants to "do nothing" about column so column just defaults to postgres. But this is just wrong because psotgres accepts nulls.

## To Reproduce
For first bug.
Create a table with not null bool column (  default true ) and remove privilege of insert.
Impersonate a user and try to insert from dashboard.
While on insert screen bool column will show up as default value.
Try inserting. It will fail because privilege doesnt exist and it tried to insert a value.

If you make bool column nullable then you can select null from dropdown menu. And you will successfully insert with default value. Because dashboard didint even tried to insert null, it just did no insert for given column.

Second one is the root of first bug and its a big one.
Basically dashboard insertion screen doesn't differentiate between NULL and "doing nothing". You can check this with text fields. Setting to null just defaults to default column value.


## Expected behavior

Dashboard insert screen should differentiate between NULL and "doing nothing". Which would actually make inserting working properly and also fix privilege bug.

title: Realtime WebSocket returns Cloudflare 1101 'Worker threw exception' — REST API works fine
description:
## Project Reference
- Project ref: `fjsptngvmcfwzqtoveud`
- Region: (default/US)
- Plan: Free tier

## Summary
Supabase Realtime WebSocket endpoint returns HTTP 500 with Cloudflare error page "Worker threw exception" (error 1101). REST API works perfectly — only Realtime is affected.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Connect to Realtime via `@supabase/supabase-js` v2.99.1:
```js
supabase
  .channel("barber-dashboard")
  .on("postgres_changes", { event: "*", schema: "public", table: "appointments" }, callback)
  .subscribe((status, err) => console.log(status, err));
```
2. Subscribe callback fires with `CHANNEL_ERROR`, then `TIMED_OUT`

## Direct endpoint test
```bash
curl -sI "https://fjsptngvmcfwzqtoveud.supabase.co/realtime/v1/websocket?apikey=<anon_key>&vsn=1.0.0"
# Returns HTTP/2 500, Cloudflare "Worker threw exception" (1101)
```

## What we've verified
- REST API (CRUD on all tables) works fine with the same anon key
- `supabase_realtime` publication includes `appointments` and `notifications` tables
- RLS: anon SELECT policy exists on both tables
- Supabase status page shows all systems operational
- Project restart did not resolve the issue
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_USE_SUPABASE` env var is clean (no trailing newline)
- Client-side logs confirm `USE_SUPABASE=true` and subscription attempts

## Expected Behavior
WebSocket connection to Realtime endpoint should succeed. Subscribe callback should fire with `SUBSCRIBED` status.

## Actual Behavior
Endpoint returns 500/Cloudflare 1101. Client receives `CHANNEL_ERROR` → `TIMED_OUT`.

## Environment
- `@supabase/supabase-js`: 2.99.1
- Framework: Next.js 16 (deployed on Vercel)
- Browser: Chrome (latest)

title: Bug: fetchMoreFolderContents uses wrong prefix in column view pagination
description:
## Bug report

### Describe the bug

In the Storage Explorer column view, `fetchMoreFolderContents` computes the API prefix using all `openedFolders` instead of slicing to the target column index. This causes "load more" pagination in non-leaf columns to fetch objects from the wrong directory.

`fetchFolderContents` correctly slices:

```typescript
// storage-explorer.tsx L335-338
const prefix = state.openedFolders
  .slice(0, index + 1)
  .map((folder) => folder.name)
  .join('/')
```

`fetchMoreFolderContents` does not:

```typescript
// storage-explorer.tsx L398
const prefix = state.openedFolders
  .map((folder) => folder.name)
  .join('/')
```

Both functions accept an `index` parameter, but `fetchMoreFolderContents` ignores it for prefix computation.

### Severity

Users see files from the wrong directory when paginating in column view. If they act on those files (download, delete, move), the operation targets wrong objects.

### To reproduce

1. Open Storage Explorer in column view
2. Create or navigate into nested folders (e.g. A → B → C)
3. Ensure the root column (or folder A) has enough items to trigger pagination (100+ items, or reduce `LIMIT` constant for testing)
4. In that non-leaf column, scroll down to trigger "load more"
5. The additional items are fetched from the deepest path (A/B/C) instead of the column's actual path

### Expected behavior

`fetchMoreFolderContents` should use `.slice(0, index + 1)` like `fetchFolderContents` does, so pagination fetches objects from the correct directory.

### Suggested fix

```diff
- const prefix = state.openedFolders.map((folder) => folder.name).join('/')
+ const prefix = state.openedFolders
+   .slice(0, index + 1)
+   .map((folder) => folder.name)
+   .join('/')
```

### System information

- Reviewed on commit `77e5180c` (master)
- File: `apps/studio/state/storage-explorer.tsx`, line 398

### Additional context

This was missed during the refactor in #43541. The refactor correctly updated `fetchFolderContents` but missed the parallel function `fetchMoreFolderContents`.

title: Bug: SMTP form shows incorrect rate limit message when already enabled
description:
## Bug report

### Describe the bug

When editing SMTP settings while custom SMTP is already enabled, the form footer displays "Rate limit for sending emails will be increased to 30". However, the rate limit is only set during the initial enable transition, so the message and the submit behavior are out of sync in this scenario.

The UI message is shown whenever `enableSmtp` is true and the form is dirty, but the `onSubmit` handler only includes `RATE_LIMIT_EMAIL_SENT: 30` in the payload when transitioning from disabled to enabled (`isEnablingSmtp`).

### Code references

UI message (always shown when SMTP enabled + form dirty):

```typescript
// SmtpForm.tsx L474
{enableSmtp ? (
  <>Rate limit for sending emails will be increased to 30...</>
) : (...)}
```

Submit handler (only sets rate limit on enable transition):

```typescript
// SmtpForm.tsx L168-171
const isEnablingSmtp = ENABLE_SMTP && !isSmtpEnabled(authConfig)
const payload = {
  ...basePayload,
  ...(isEnablingSmtp && { RATE_LIMIT_EMAIL_SENT: 30 }),
}
```

### To reproduce

1. Go to Authentication → SMTP Settings
2. Enable custom SMTP and save (rate limit is set to 30)
3. Edit any SMTP field (e.g. change host or port)
4. Observe footer message: "Rate limit for sending emails will be increased to 30"
5. Save — the rate limit is NOT changed (already enabled, so `isEnablingSmtp = false`)

### Expected behavior

When SMTP is already enabled and the user edits other settings, the footer should not mention rate limit changes. For example:

- Enabling SMTP: "Rate limit for sending emails will be increased to 30"
- Already enabled, editing settings: (no rate limit message, or "Custom SMTP settings will be updated")
- Disabling SMTP: "Rate limit for sending emails will be reduced to 2"

### Suggested fix

There are two possible directions — happy to hear which is intended:

**Option A**: Fix the message to match the submit logic (message-side fix):

```diff
-{enableSmtp ? (
+{enableSmtp && !isSmtpEnabled(authConfig) ? (
   <>Rate limit for sending emails will be increased to 30...</>
+) : enableSmtp ? (
+  <>Custom SMTP settings will be updated</>
 ) : (
   'Rate limit for sending emails will be reduced to 2...'
 )}
```

**Option B**: Fix the submit logic to match the message (always reset rate limit to 30 on save):

```diff
-...(isEnablingSmtp && { RATE_LIMIT_EMAIL_SENT: 30 }),
+...(ENABLE_SMTP && { RATE_LIMIT_EMAIL_SENT: 30 }),
```

### System information

- Reviewed on commit `77e5180c` (master)
- File: `apps/studio/components/interfaces/Auth/SmtpForm/SmtpForm.tsx`

title: Allow newlines in SMS OTP template
description:
# Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

I'm using the SMS OTP feature for a progressive web application to be used on mobiles and would like to use the [WebOTP API](https://wicg.github.io/web-otp/) to automatically handle the SMS. The new templating feature gets me almost there – but only almost since it does not allow newlines (\n).

## Describe the solution you'd like

To conform to the WebOTP API, the SMS needs to contain at least one newline, see e.g. [here](https://web.dev/web-otp/#format) or [here](https://wicg.github.io/sms-one-time-codes/#origin-bound-one-time-code-message). The template should allow for newlines.

## Describe alternatives you've considered

There currently is no alternative to WebOTP for automated SMS OTP handling.

## Additional context

I've tried to sneak a newline into the template via "\n" and "\\\n", but to no avail.

title: Using Supabase transaction pooler, a serializable transaction can silently discard all its writes
description:
# Bug report

I can't find an existing bug report for this, am actually quite amazed by that.

## Describe the bug

When using the Supabase **transaction pooler** (PgBouncer, port 6543) with serializable isolation, a transaction with serializable isolation level can silently discard all its writes. No error is thrown, no indication of failure, and returns a fully populated row, but NO rows are persisted to the database.

## To Reproduce

I built this repo with a minimal reproduction of the bug. It is the same essential test I wrote to prove the bug in our codebase:
https://github.com/brianmgray/supabase-pool-bug-repro

Instructions in the README.md

You will need to connect it to a Supabase project, and provide the session pool URL and the transaction pool URL in a .env file, then run: 

```
corepack enable
pnpm install
pnpm db:migrate
pnpm test:session # should fail
pnpm test:transaction # should succeed
```

## Expected behavior

A database transaction should have exactly two outcomes: commit (data persists, no error) or abort (error thrown, no data written). 

With the Supabase transaction pooler under serializable isolation, a third unexpected outcome occurs: the transaction appears to commit (no error, RETURNING returns data) but all writes are silently discarded. This should never be possible.

## Screenshots

Sample output:

```sh
❯ pnpm test:session

> supabase-pool-bug-repro@1.0.0 test:session /Users/brian/workspaces/fuguUX/archive/supabase-pool-bug-repro
> POOL=session node repro.js


--- Session Pooler (port 5432) ---

=== Phase 1: READ COMMITTED allows overdraft ===

  ℹ️  Only 1 hold succeeded — transactions serialized by timing

=== Phase 2: SERIALIZABLE + INSERT...RETURNING ghost rows ===

  Serialization failures: 6 (expected — SSI working)
  Apparent commits (RETURNING returned data): 2
  ✅ All 2 committed rows verified — no bugs found
```

```sh
❯ pnpm test:transaction

> supabase-pool-bug-repro@1.0.0 test:transaction /Users/brian/workspaces/fuguUX/archive/supabase-pool-bug-repro
> POOL=transaction node repro.js


--- Transaction Pooler (port 6543) ---

=== Phase 1: READ COMMITTED allows overdraft ===

  ℹ️  Only 1 hold succeeded — transactions serialized by timing

=== Phase 2: SERIALIZABLE + INSERT...RETURNING ghost rows ===

  🐛 BUG: INSERT...RETURNING returned idempotency_key=hold-ssi-3-af7aa9f6-2f35-46c7-b67e-bb6a8a1cbdd6 but the row is NOT FOUND after commit
  Serialization failures: 5 (expected — SSI working)
  Apparent commits (RETURNING returned data): 3
  🐛 1 of 3 rows NOT FOUND after commit  ← the bug
```

## System information

- OS: MacOS
- Browser (if applies): n/a
- Version of supabase-js: n/a, using postgres and in our app drizzle (same result)
- Version of Node.js: v22.22.0

## Additional context

None

title: ci: Add workflow_dispatch trigger to enable manual CI runs
description:
# Chore

## Describe the chore

Add `workflow_dispatch:` trigger to the core GitHub Actions CI workflow files to enable manual workflow runs from the GitHub Actions UI.

The following workflow files currently only trigger on `push` and `pull_request` events and lack the ability to be triggered manually:

- [.github/workflows/studio-unit-tests.yml](cci:7://file:///Users/mac/Desktop/supabase/.github/workflows/studio-unit-tests.yml:0:0-0:0)
- [.github/workflows/typecheck.yml](cci:7://file:///Users/mac/Desktop/supabase/.github/workflows/typecheck.yml:0:0-0:0)
- [.github/workflows/ui-tests.yml](cci:7://file:///Users/mac/Desktop/supabase/.github/workflows/ui-tests.yml:0:0-0:0)
- [.github/workflows/ui-patterns-tests.yml](cci:7://file:///Users/mac/Desktop/supabase/.github/workflows/ui-patterns-tests.yml:0:0-0:0)
- [.github/workflows/www-tests.yml](cci:7://file:///Users/mac/Desktop/supabase/.github/workflows/www-tests.yml:0:0-0:0)
- [.github/workflows/docs-tests.yml](cci:7://file:///Users/mac/Desktop/supabase/.github/workflows/docs-tests.yml:0:0-0:0)
- [.github/workflows/ai-tests.yml](cci:7://file:///Users/mac/Desktop/supabase/.github/workflows/ai-tests.yml:0:0-0:0)
- [.github/workflows/pg-meta-tests.yml](cci:7://file:///Users/mac/Desktop/supabase/.github/workflows/pg-meta-tests.yml:0:0-0:0)

The fix is to add `workflow_dispatch:` to the `on:` block of each file, for example:

```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master]
  workflow_dispatch:   # ← add this

description:
# Bug report

## Describe the bug

Custom SMTP does not work with MS Office 365 or Outlook (smtp.office365.com or smtp.outlook.com)

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository:

1. Go to 'Authentication'
2. Setup Custom SMTP for office/outlook
3. Run any built-in email functions like (Confirm signup, Invite user, Magic Link, Reset Password etc.) 
For example: 
```
const { error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
    email,
    password,
    options: {
      emailRedirectTo: `${origin}/auth/callback`,
    },
  });
```

4. Error received: "504: Processing this request timed out, please retry after a moment."

## Expected behavior

Successfully send email to user using custom smtp and sender email

## Screenshots

**Example Custom SMTP Setup:**
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e2195899-5f8c-41da-9f0f-f41607802484)


## System information

- Browser (if applies) Chrome, Firefox
- Version of supabase-js: 2.49.1

title: Supabase MCP OAuth fails with {"message":"Unrecognized client_id"} in Cursor
description:
## Bug Report

**Summary:** When using the official Supabase MCP server in Cursor, the OAuth flow fails immediately with `{"message":"Unrecognized client_id"}` when the browser is redirected to mcp.supabase.com, preventing MCP authentication from completing.

---

### MCP Config (`.cursor/mcp.json`)

```json
{
  "supabase": {
    "type": "http",
    "url": "https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp"
  }
}
```

---

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Add the above MCP config to Cursor.
2. Open Cursor and wait for the Supabase MCP server to initialize.
3. Click **Authenticate** when Cursor prompts for MCP authentication.
4. Browser opens to the Supabase OAuth page.
5. Immediately see `{"message":"Unrecognized client_id"}` returned from `mcp.supabase.com`.

---

### Expected Behavior

Clicking **Authenticate** for the Supabase MCP in Cursor should:
1. Open the browser to Supabase
2. Allow project selection
3. Return a valid token to Cursor so MCP tools can query the project

---

### Actual Behavior

- Browser opens to Supabase and immediately shows: `{"message":"Unrecognized client_id"}`
- Cursor reports: `Tool rejected: User skipped MCP authentication`
- Cursor reports: `Authentication timed out after 2 minutes`
- The OAuth flow never completes and Cursor never receives a token
- This cannot be resolved by modifying `mcp.json` or rotating API keys — the issue appears to be that the `client_id` being used by the MCP app is not registered/recognized on `mcp.supabase.com`

---

### Environment

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Editor | Cursor |
| Supabase Project URL | https://rumdmulxzmjtsplsjngi.supabase.co |
| MCP Server URL | https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp |
| Supabase MCP Plugin Version | `release_v0.1.3` (from `.cursor/plugins/cache/cursor-public/supabase/release_v0.1.3/mcp.json`) |

---

### Notes

- Direct SQL execution in Supabase Studio works fine; the issue is isolated to the MCP OAuth flow.
- Reference/repro project: `BruceTyndall/socelle-global` (if needed for investigation)
- This appears to be a server-side OAuth client registration issue, not a client-side configuration problem.

title: PostgREST schema cache not updating - PGRST204 errors
description:
# Bug report

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug

PostgREST schema cache does not update when new columns are added to existing tables or when new tables are created. The cache persists stale information despite all documented refresh methods. This makes it impossible to extend the database schema in production.

**Error Code:** `PGRST204`  
**Error Message:** `"Could not find the 'column_name' column of 'table_name' in the schema cache"`

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. **Add a new column to existing table:**
```sql
ALTER TABLE planting_plans ADD COLUMN batch_number TEXT;
```

2. **Verify column exists in database:**
```sql
SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns 
WHERE table_name='planting_plans' AND column_name='batch_number';
-- Returns: batch_number ✓
```

3. **Attempt to use new column via PostgREST API:**
```javascript
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('planting_plans')
  .insert({ batch_number: 'TEST-123', /* other fields */ });
```

4. **See error:**
```json
{
  "code": "PGRST204",
  "message": "Could not find the 'batch_number' column of 'planting_plans' in the schema cache"
}
```

**Attempted cache refresh methods (NONE worked):**
```sql
-- Method 1
NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';

-- Method 2
NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload config';
SELECT pg_reload_conf();

-- Method 3
GRANT ALL ON TABLE planting_plans TO anon, authenticated, service_role;
NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';

-- Method 4: DROP and recreate
ALTER TABLE planting_plans DROP COLUMN batch_number;
ALTER TABLE planting_plans ADD COLUMN batch_number TEXT;
GRANT ALL ON TABLE planting_plans TO anon, authenticated, service_role;
NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';

-- Method 5: Project restart via Dashboard
-- Paused project → Resumed → Still broken
```

**Also tested:** Creating columns via Supabase Dashboard GUI (Table Editor) - same result.

**Also tested:** Creating entirely new table - table not visible to PostgREST at all (404).

## Expected behavior

When a column is added via `ALTER TABLE` or Supabase GUI:
- PostgREST cache should automatically update within seconds
- `NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema'` should force immediate cache refresh
- New columns should be immediately usable via API

When project is paused/resumed:
- PostgREST cache should be completely cleared and rebuilt

## Screenshots

(Attach screenshots showing PGRST204 errors from browser console)

Error from browser DevTools Console:
```
POST https://yyigfdevvikroeenqott.supabase.co/rest/v1/planting_plans
Status: 400 (Bad Request)

{
  "code": "PGRST204",
  "details": null,
  "hint": null,
  "message": "Could not find the 'batch_number' column of 'planting_plans' in the schema cache"
}
```

## System information

- **OS:** Windows 11
- **Browser:** Chrome 120+
- **Version of supabase-js:** Latest (@supabase/supabase-js)
- **Version of Node.js:** N/A (using Supabase client in browser)
- **Supabase Project:** 
  - Plan: Free
  - Project ID: `pdteqherbfpndfjpghvh`
  - URL: `yyigfdevvikroeenqott.supabase.co`
- **PostgREST Version:** (Unknown - using Supabase hosted)

## Additional context

**Timeline:**
- **Jan 25, 2026:** First attempted to add columns via SQL - cache error appeared
- **Jan 25-26:** Spent 3+ hours trying every documented solution
- **Jan 26:** Tried GUI column creation - still fails with PGRST204

**Impact:**
- Blocks all database schema changes
- Cannot add new features requiring new columns
- Development completely halted
- Workarounds exhausted

**What works:**
- Existing columns and tables work perfectly
- Direct PostgreSQL queries work
- Only PostgREST API layer affected

**Columns that won't appear in cache:**
- `batch_number` (text)
- `test_notes` (text)
- `test_type` (text)
- Previously: `include_in_stats` (boolean) - also failed

**Other observations:**
- RLS is properly configured
- Permissions are granted to all roles
- Schema is `public`
- No special characters in column names
- Column types are standard (text, boolean)

This appears to be a persistent bug in PostgREST cache invalidation mechanism for our specific project. Manual cache flush or investigation needed.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5c0d3cf3-f14a-4721-9619-1bf36fc8d891)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b9b1258e-f39b-468b-815b-2fb19285bb67)

title: Docs: clarify connection mode for local psql imports in CSV ingestion workflows
description:
I’m running a local CSV normalization + ingest workflow into Supabase Postgres.

### Workflow
1. Parse jobs from local CSV/ZIP sources.
2. Prepare schema and `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`.
3. Import via `psql` using Supabase connection details.

### Problem
The SQL path is fine, but the workflow gets blocked at connection setup because the docs for import and connection mode are split across different pages.
In practice, it’s easy to pick the wrong connection path for local `psql` import unless you already know the connection-mode caveats.

### Expected behavior
A single import flow should clearly state:
1. Which connection string/host is recommended for local `psql` import.
2. When that path may fail depending on network/setup.
3. The fallback path to use next.
4. A minimal verified command example.

### Actual behavior
I reached a dead-end before SQL execution because the connection details were incomplete/misaligned for my local environment, and I had to infer the correct mode from scattered docs.

### Why this matters
This affects practical ingestion pipelines (CSV normalization, dedupe, upsert) where users need to get from local files to database writes quickly. The current docs make that first connection step easier to get wrong than it should be.

### Suggested docs improvement
Add a short "Connection mode decision" section in the import guide:
1. If local `psql`, start with X
2. If X fails, use Y
3. Known caveats
4. Minimal end-to-end example

title: Incorrect code sample in Reverse Proxy and HTTPS Guide
description:
# Improve documentation

## Link 

https://supabase.com/docs/guides/self-hosting/self-hosted-proxy-https

## Describe the problem

I followed the Configure Reverse Proxy and HTTPS How-to Guide, and I commented out the ports as indicated.

```
kong:
  # ...
  ports:
    # - ${KONG_HTTP_PORT}:8000/tcp
    # - ${KONG_HTTPS_PORT}:8443/tcp
```

The example comments out the port numbers, but not the `ports:` line, and I got an error from it.

```
validating /opt/supabase-project/docker-compose.yml: services.kong.ports must be a array
```

## Describe the improvement

To solve this error, I also commented out the `ports:` line.

```
kong:
  # ports:
    # - ${KONG_HTTP_PORT}:8000/tcp
    # - ${KONG_HTTPS_PORT}:8443/tcp
```

Since there are no other ports listed in the default `docker-compose.yml`, someone using the guide will likely have to comment out the `ports:` line too.

## Additional context
I did not use the Self-signed certificates section, so I don't know whether the same ports should be commented out there, but that section might have the same problem.

Thanks so much for this guide! It helped me a lot.

title: Frozen Supabase/auth-js navigator.locks.request()
description:
We are having a problem where login just gets stuck and times out.                                                         
   
  The Supabase JS client (@supabase/auth-js) uses navigator.locks.request()     
  internally for all auth operations. This lock can enter a "zombie" state where
   it's never released — particularly on mobile/PWA, after tab backgrounding, or
   after process kills. When this happens, every call through the Supabase   
  client hangs forever: signInWithPassword(), getSession(), setSession(),
  database queries — all of them. 
                                      
  
For our build,  both /admin/login and /employer/login call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword().
   This method internally calls _request() (the actual HTTP call, which works
  fine) but then calls _saveSession() and _notifyAllSubscribers() which go      
  through the lock. If the lock is stuck, the call never resolves and the    
  spinner spins forever.

  The 30-second Promise.race timeout in employer login should catch this, but   
  the underlying problem remains — even if the timeout fires, the Supabase
  client is still frozen for all subsequent operations.                         
                                                                             

  Even if login somehow succeeds:                                               
  - EmployerGate calls supabase.functions.invoke('get-employer-access') — hangs
  on frozen client                                                              
  - useAdmin calls fetch() directly (works), but AdminLayout also uses       
  useSupabaseAuth which relies on onAuthStateChange — may never fire if client  
  is frozen                                                                     
  - Dashboard pages query supabase.from('employers').select(...) — hangs on
  frozen client                                                                 
  - getStoredSession() in authStorage.ts only recognizes {currentSession: ...}  
  or {session: ...} wrapper format, not the direct session format the Supabase
  client actually stores — so on reload, it can't find the session              
                                                                             
 Does anyone know of a fix for this?

title: RLS INSERT policy WITH CHECK (true) TO PUBLIC blocks anon role via PostgREST API despite succeeding with SET ROLE anon in SQL Editor
description:
# Bug report

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application.
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [[Docs](https://docs.supabase.com/)](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [[Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions)](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [[Discord](https://discord.supabase.com/)](https://discord.supabase.com).

## Describe the bug

A PERMISSIVE RLS INSERT policy with `WITH CHECK (true)` applied `TO PUBLIC` (all roles) is still blocking INSERT operations for the `anon` role, returning error `42501: new row violates row-level security policy for table "orders"`.

The policy is correctly visible in `pg_policies` and all necessary GRANTs are in place. The same INSERT succeeds when RLS is disabled, and also succeeds when running `SET ROLE anon` directly in the SQL Editor — meaning the issue is specific to requests coming through the PostgREST API with an anon JWT.

## To Reproduce

1. Create a table with RLS enabled:
```sql
CREATE TABLE public.orders (
  id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
  restaurant_id uuid NOT NULL,
  customer_email text NOT NULL,
  total_price numeric NOT NULL
);

ALTER TABLE public.orders ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
```

2. Create a fully permissive INSERT policy for all roles:
```sql
CREATE POLICY "allow all insert"
ON public.orders
AS PERMISSIVE
FOR INSERT
TO PUBLIC
WITH CHECK (true);
```

3. Grant INSERT to anon:
```sql
GRANT INSERT ON public.orders TO anon;
GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA public TO anon;
```

4. Send a POST request via the PostgREST API using the anon JWT:
```js
fetch('https://YOUR_PROJECT.supabase.co/rest/v1/orders', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    'apikey': 'ANON_KEY',
    'Authorization': 'Bearer ANON_KEY',
    'Prefer': 'return=representation'
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    restaurant_id: 'VALID_UUID',
    customer_email: 'test@gmail.com',
    total_price: 10.00
  })
})
```

5. Receive `42501: new row violates row-level security policy for table "orders"`

## Expected behavior

The INSERT should succeed since the policy is `TO PUBLIC WITH CHECK (true)`, which should allow any role including `anon` to insert any row.

## Additional context

- Running `SET ROLE anon` in the SQL Editor and performing the same INSERT succeeds without error — confirming the policy is correctly defined at the database level.
- Disabling RLS (`ALTER TABLE orders DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`) makes the API request succeed.
- The `debug_current_role()` function called via RPC from the browser returns `anon`, confirming the correct role is being used.
- The anon JWT decodes correctly with `"role": "anon"`.
- All policies are `PERMISSIVE`, none are `RESTRICTIVE`.
- GRANTs are confirmed via `information_schema.role_table_grants`.
- Workaround used: moved the INSERT to a Supabase Edge Function using the `service_role` key.

## System information

- OS: windows
- Browser: Chrome
- Version of supabase-js: 2.x
- PostgreSQL: 17.6 on aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu

title: Free plan database size is under disk size in docs "Understanding Database and Disk Size" causes confusion.
description:
# Improve/Fix documentation

## The Problem
Below problem is about free plan but this problem may also exist on paid plans.

On [Supabase Pricing Web Page](https://supabase.com/pricing)
Free plan has no mention of _disk size_ but **database size** which is "500 MB **database size** per project included".
Row name is **Database Size** but paid plans use term _disk size_.

On [Project Settings: compute and disk](https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/_/settings/compute-and-disk)
At _disk size_ it says "Your plan includes 1 GB of _disk size_ for gp3." for free plan.

On [Organization Usage: **database size**](https://supabase.com/dashboard/org/_/usage#dbSize)
It says "Included in Free Plan 0.5 GB per project" which is under **database size**

On [Docs: Understanding Database and Disk Size](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/platform/database-size)
At [#free-plan-behavior](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/platform/database-size#free-plan-behavior)
It says "Free Plan projects enter read-only mode when you exceed the 500 MB limit." Which is wrong ( this free plan explanation is under _Disk size_ -> Free Plan behaviour ) according to above information. 
Also there is no mention of free plan at **database size** section.

I believe correct one is **database size** at [Organization Usage: **database size**](https://supabase.com/dashboard/org/_/usage#dbSize)

description:
Problem

Supabase platform removed `app.settings.jwt_secret` from the database in November 2024 (see discussion [#30606](https://github.com/orgs/supabase/discussions/30606)) for security reasons - it leaks the JWT signing secret to anyone with database access, bypassing role-based access controls.

[Self-hosted Supabase](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/self-hosting) still sets it via two mechanisms:

1. Database-level default - in `docker/volumes/db/jwt.sql` via `ALTER DATABASE postgres SET "app.settings.jwt_secret" TO :'jwt_secret';`
2. PostgREST per-transaction override - `PGRST_APP_SETTINGS_JWT_SECRET` in `docker/docker-compose.yml`, which runs `SET LOCAL  "app.settings.jwt_secret"` on every PostgREST transaction.

No bundled SQL function in the self-hosted setup actually reads this value - it exists only for user-written functions (e.g., using `pgjwt` to sign/verify JWTs via `current_setting('app.settings.jwt_secret')`).

Self-hosted should align with platform and remove `app.settings.jwt_secret`. Users who need the JWT secret in SQL should use https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/vault.

title: Clarify when to use `getClaims` and `getUser` and discourage usage of `getSession`
description:
### Documentation is

- [x] Missing
- [x] Outdated
- [ ] Confusing
- [ ] Not sure?

### Explain in Detail

All of the Server Framework Documentation still uses `getUser()` by default, which means there is an extraneous call to the database. Supabase created `getClaims()` for this purpose increasing the speed and security of Supabase Auth. 

The docs should reflect this by default.

### Outdated Setup
- [NextJS](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/tutorials/with-nextjs?queryGroups=database-method&database-method=sql&queryGroups=language&language=ts) - see middleware.ts
- [SvelteKit](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/tutorials/with-sveltekit?queryGroups=database-method&database-method=sql) - see hooks.server.ts

### No Server Setup At All
- [Nuxt](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/tutorials/with-nuxt-3?queryGroups=database-method&database-method=sql) - this doesn't even have a server endpoint setup and is just a Vue setup
- [SolidJS](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/tutorials/with-solidjs?queryGroups=database-method&database-method=sql) - There should be a `SolidStart` version for server

The [@supabase/ssr]() makes it easy to add functionality to any framework, and could easily be used in place of outdated addons like:
- [Qwik Supabase](https://qwik.dev/docs/integrations/supabase/#supabase)
- [Nuxt Supabase](https://supabase.nuxtjs.org/)

I am happy to help create a demo for all of these if necessary (just let me know), but first *SvelteKit* and *NextJS* docs need to be updated with `getClaims()` instead of `getUser()`, and before `getClaims()` can be useful, the [typing problem](https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/issues/1584) should be fixed.

J

## Describe the bug
Users attempting to sign in via Supabase Auth from a network in Laos are unable to complete the login flow. Requests hang or return a network error. If the user uses a VPN to a different location on the same device, it works.

## To Reproduce

1. Use a mobile device within Laos (example IP 154.222.5.99)
2. Calls `supabase.auth.signIn({ email, password })` or `supabase.auth.signUp({ email, password })`.  
3. Enter valid credentials and submit.  
4. Observe that the Promise neither resolves nor rejects; the browser console logs a network timeout

## Expected behavior
Authentication requests should succeed regardless of the user’s country or ISP. Users in Laos should see the same login experience as users in other regions.

## Screenshots
_No UI error is shown; the network tab simply shows the request pending until it times out._

## System information

- React Native version: 0.74.5
- Expo SDK: 51
- Supabase Version: 2.43.5
- Device: iPhone 13 Pro Max (D64AP)
- OS version: iOS 18.4.1

- This happens for several different users in Laos, confirmed it happens only in Laos

```
__isAuthError: true,
message: Network request timed out,
name: AuthRetryableFetchError
```

title: Supabase API Key Migration Issues with Type Generation and FlutterFlow
description:
# Bug report
Supabase API Key Migration Issues with Type Generation and FlutterFlow
​Bug Summary:
After migrating from the deprecated Supabase "anon key" to the new "publishable key" ( sb_publishable_... format), two distinct issues arose:
​Database Type Generation Failure (React Application): In one React application, the Supabase CLI's database type generation command (npx supabase gen types typescript --project-id "$PROJECT_REF" --schema public > database.types.ts) continues to expect the old "anon key" despite the project being configured with the new "publishable key." This prevents type generation unless the "anon key" is temporarily re-enabled.
​FlutterFlow Connection Failure: A FlutterFlow application is unable to establish a connection to Supabase using the new "publishable key." The connection fails, preventing data interaction from within FlutterFlow.

<!-- 

⚠️ We receive a lot of bug reports which have already been solved or discussed. If you are looking for help, please try these first:

- Docs: https://docs.supabase.com
- Discussions: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions
- Discord: https://discord.supabase.com

Before opening a bug report, please verify the following: 

-->

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug

After migrating from the deprecated Supabase "anon key" to the new "publishable key" ( sb_publishable_... format), two distinct issues arose:
​Database Type Generation Failure (React Application): In one React application, the Supabase CLI's database type generation command (npx supabase gen types typescript --project-id "$PROJECT_REF" --schema public > database.types.ts) continues to expect the old "anon key" despite the project being configured with the new "publishable key." This prevents type generation unless the "anon key" is temporarily re-enabled.
​FlutterFlow Connection Failure: A FlutterFlow application is unable to establish a connection to Supabase using the new "publishable key." The connection fails, preventing data interaction from within FlutterFlow.


## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository:

 1. In the Supabase Dashboard, navigate to Project Settings > API Keys.
 2. Ensure the "anon key" is disabled.
 3. Ensure a "publishable key" ( sb_publishable_...) is active.
 4. In the React application's root directory, run the Supabase CLI command for type generation:
   npx supabase gen types typescript --project-id "YOUR_PROJECT_REF" --schema public > database.types.ts

 5. Observe the error message indicating an expectation of the "anon key" (exact error message would be helpful here).
 6. As a workaround, re-enable the "anon key" in the Supabase Dashboard.
 7. Re-run the type generation command.
 8. Observe that type generation now succeeds.
Steps to Reproduce (FlutterFlow Application):
 1. In the Supabase Dashboard, navigate to Project Settings > API Keys.
 2. Ensure the "anon key" is disabled.
 3. Ensure a "publishable key" ( sb_publishable_...) is active.
 4. In FlutterFlow, attempt to configure or connect to the Supabase project using the new "publishable key."
 5. Attempt to fetch data or perform any database operation from within FlutterFlow that relies on the Supabase connection.
 6. Observe the connection failure or error message within FlutterFlow (exact error message or behavior would be helpful here).

## Expected behavior

 1. React Application: The Supabase CLI should be able to generate database types using the "publishable key" without requiring the "anon key" to be enabled.
 2. FlutterFlow Application: FlutterFlow should successfully connect to the Supabase project and allow data interaction using the "publishable key."

## Screenshots

If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

No need, the problem is clear.

## System information

- OS: [linux, windows]
- Browser (if applies) [chrome]
- Version of supabase-js: [latest]
- Version of Node.js: not needed

## Additional context
This issue seems to indicate that certain Supabase tooling (CLI for type generation) and integrations (FlutterFlow) may not be fully compatible with or recognize the new "publishable key" as a direct replacement for the "anon key" in all scenarios, despite Supabase's documentation suggesting it should be.

title: Regression: .eq() filter lacks type safety for column names
before-user-created webhook sends different ID than is actually solved in Auth for SSO providers
description:
# Bug report

- [ x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [ x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug

The before-user-created Auth Hook sends a different user.id in the webhook payload than what Supabase Auth actually stores in auth.users when users sign up via OAuth/SSO providers (confirmed with Google OAuth).
This makes the webhook unreliable for its primary use case: creating related records (e.g., profiles) with foreign key relationships to auth.users.id.


## To Reproduce

1. Configure a before-user-created webhook endpoint per [docs](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/auth-hooks/before-user-created-hook)
2. In your webhook handler, log or store the user.id from the payload
3. Sign up a new user using Google OAuth
4. Query auth.users for the newly created user
5. Compare the id from the webhook payload with the id in auth.users
6. Observe they do not match

Note: Email+password signup works correctly—the IDs match. The issue only occurs with OAuth/SSO providers.


## Expected behavior

The user.id provided in the before-user-created webhook payload should match the id that gets stored in auth.users, regardless of the authentication method used.

title: Prisma no longer supports `url` and `directUrl` in `schema.prisma`, forcing DB connection to be defined in code and breaking Supabase migration flow
description:
In the latest Prisma version (7.2.0), the database connection can no longer be defined inside schema.prisma using DATABASE_URL.

Previously, Prisma allowed defining the database connection directly in the schema file:

```prisma
datasource db {
  generator client {
    provider = "prisma-client-js"
  }
  
  datasource db {
    provider  = "postgresql"
    url       = env("DATABASE_URL")
    directUrl = env("DIRECT_URL")
  }
 }
```

However, this no longer works in the current version. The schema now requires the datasource to not include url and directUrl, and the database connection must instead be defined directly when instantiating PrismaClient in application code.

Example of the current required setup:
``` typescript
import { PrismaPg } from '@prisma/adapter-pg'
import { PrismaClient } from '../generated/prisma/client'

const adapter = new PrismaPg({ connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL })
const prisma = new PrismaClient({ adapter })
```

Since Supabase still requires two separate database URLs, one for application runtime (pooled connection) and another for migrations (direct connection), this change creates a significant workflow issue.

Currently, `prisma.config.ts` relies on `DATABASE_URL`:
```typescript
datasource: {
  url: process.env["DATABASE_URL"],
}
```

However, when running:
```bash
npx prisma migrate
```
the command fails to migrate (stuck) because Supabase does not allow migrations using the pooled connection. Migrations must use the direct connection URL instead.

As a result, developers are forced to manually replace `DATABASE_URL` with `DIRECT_URL` in the configuration whenever running migrations. This is error-prone, breaks automation, and defeats the purpose of having separate connection types.

This behavior makes Prisma’s current workflow incompatible with Supabase’s recommended connection setup

title: Native Sign in with Apple fails — OIDC issuer mismatch
description:
Subject: Native Sign in with Apple fails — OIDC issuer mismatch
Project ref: zjwzvpincdmjrdlfmvdw
Description:
Native Sign in with Apple on iOS is failing with "Internal Server Error" (500) when calling signInWithIdToken from the Supabase Swift SDK.
This appears to be the known OIDC issuer mismatch issue (GitHub: supabase/auth#2051) where Apple changed their ID token issuer from https://appleid.apple.com to https://account.apple.com, but Supabase Auth still validates against the old issuer.
The fix was rolled out to most projects in June 2025, but our project still appears to be affected. We confirmed:
The Apple provider is correctly configured (Client ID, Team ID, Key ID, Secret Key)
Sign in with Apple capability is enabled in Xcode and the Apple Developer Portal
The native Apple sign-in sheet appears and completes successfully on the device
The error occurs when the token is sent to Supabase for validation
Email/password signup works correctly on this project
Could you please apply the OIDC issuer fix to our project?
Thank you.

[x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application.
[x] I confirm I have searched the Docs, GitHub Discussions, and Discord.

Describe the bug

Native Sign in with Apple fails with "Internal Server Error" (HTTP 500) when calling signInWithIdToken from the Supabase Swift SDK. This is the known OIDC issuer mismatch issue where Apple's ID tokens use a different issuer URL than what Supabase Auth expects.
The fix was merged in supabase/auth#2068 (Auth v2.177.0, July 2025) but does not appear to be applied to my project. A project restart did not resolve the issue.
Related issues: supabase/auth#2051, supabase/supabase#36319
To Reproduce
Configure Apple provider in Supabase Dashboard (Client ID, Team ID, Key ID, Secret Key)
In an iOS app using the Supabase Swift SDK, use SignInWithAppleButton from AuthenticationServices
Complete the native Apple sign-in flow on the device
Call client.auth.signInWithIdToken(credentials: .init(provider: .apple, idToken: identityToken, nonce: nonce))
Receive HTTP 500: "Internal Server Error"
Expected behavior
The sign-in should succeed, returning a valid session. The native Apple sign-in sheet should complete and the user should be authenticated.
Screenshots
Auth logs show:
/token | server_error: Internal Server Error
System information
OS: macOS / iOS 18
Supabase project ref: zjwzvpincdmjrdlfmvdw
Supabase Swift SDK: latest (via Swift Package Manager)
Xcode: 16.x
Sign in with Apple: Capability enabled, entitlements configured
Additional context
Email/password signup works correctly on this project (confirmed via curl)
Google OAuth works correctly
The Apple provider is enabled and configured in the Supabase Dashboard
The project was restarted via Dashboard → Settings → General → Restart project — issue persists
The fix in supabase/auth#2068 appears to resolve this by reverting the default OIDC issuer to appleid.apple.com. Please apply this to my project.

title: OAuth App creation UI lacks logo URL input; long auto-generated logo URL fails with 2048-char limit
description:
## Summary

When creating an OAuth App in the Supabase Dashboard (**Organization Settings → OAuth Apps → Add application**), the form only allows uploading a logo **file** — there is no option to paste a logo URL directly. The Dashboard uploads the image and stores a hosted (often signed) URL. For some uploads, the resulting URL exceeds common length limits (~2048 characters), causing the create/update request to fail.

This prevents configuring a logo during creation when the auto-generated URL is too long. The only workaround is to host the logo at a short public URL and then update the OAuth client via the Auth Admin API to set `logo_uri` explicitly.

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to **Organization Settings → OAuth Apps**
2. Click **"Add application"**
3. Fill in required fields and upload a logo image file
4. Submit the form

---

## Observed Behavior

- No option to paste a logo URL directly in the form
- For some uploads, the operation fails or the resulting `logo_uri` is rejected due to length (likely exceeding ~2048 characters)

---

## Expected Behavior

- Ability to paste a short logo URL directly (a **"Paste URL"** option alongside the file upload), **or**
- The Dashboard emits a stable, short `logo_uri` that won't exceed typical URL length limits
- Clear UI messaging or documentation about `logo_uri` constraints

---

## Workarounds Tested

**Option 1:** Host the image at a short public URL (e.g., Supabase Storage public bucket with a short path like `brand/splash.png`)

**Option 2:** Use the Auth Admin API to update the client after creation:

```bash
curl -X PATCH 'https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1/admin/oauth/clients/<client_id>' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <SERVICE_ROLE_KEY>' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{ "logo_uri": "https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/brand/splash.png" }'
```

✅ Result: Successfully sets a short `logo_uri` and avoids the error.

---

## Impact

- **Blocks** OAuth App creation/editing with a logo when the auto-generated URL is too long
- **Adds operational overhead** — forces developers to use the Admin API for a task that should be possible in the UI
- **Causes confusion** — no clear UI or documentation guidance on `logo_uri` constraints or supported input methods

---

## Requested Resolution

## Describe the bug

It's not currently possible to import a CSV file in Supabase Studio with a genuine empty string value, which is not the same as a NULL value. It treats empty cells as null, but it also treats NULL as null, so there's two ways to enter a null value and no way to enter an empty string.

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository:

1. Go to Supabase Studio.
2. Open the table editor with some table.
3. Insert -> import CSV and import a file with some empty cells.
4. See that the imported data has empty cells set to NULL (even in the preview).

## Expected behavior

It should treat empty cells as empty strings or at least treat EMPTY as empty strings (which is how they're displayed in the table editor). But in the latter case you'd have to have a way to escape that to actually enter the value "EMPTY".

title: REST API ignores PostgREST CORS settings, forcing Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
description:
# Bug report

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug

Supabase appears to override the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header at the Cloudflare proxy level, forcing it to `*`, even when PostgREST is explicitly configured with a strict allowed origin.

This makes it impossible to enforce strict CORS policies on the Supabase REST API, which is a requirement raised during a penetration test.

## To Reproduce

1. Attempt to configure strict CORS for the Supabase REST API.
2. Discover that Supabase does not expose a CORS configuration option for the REST API.
3. Apply the documented PostgREST configuration directly via SQL:

```sql
ALTER ROLE authenticator
SET pgrst.server_cors_allowed_origins = 'https://foo.example';

NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload config';
```

4. Confirm that the PostgREST configuration is successfully updated.
5. Make a request to the Supabase REST API from a browser.
6. Observe that the response header `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` is still set to `*`.

## Expected behavior

When `pgrst.server_cors_allowed_origins` is set to a specific origin, the Supabase REST API should return that exact value in the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` response header, rather than `*`.

This would allow developers to enforce strict CORS policies as required by security audits and penetration tests.

## Screenshots

Not applicable.

## System information

- OS: Not relevant (issue occurs server-side)
- Browser (if applies): Any (observed via browser network inspection)
- Version of supabase-js: Not applicable
- Version of Node.js: Not applicable

## Additional context

- The PostgREST configuration change is confirmed to take effect internally.
- The `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` response appears to be injected or overridden by Supabase’s Cloudflare proxy layer.
- This behavior prevents compliance with security requirements that explicitly disallow wildcard CORS origins.

title: plpgsql_check causing "cannot find parent statement on pldbgapi2 call stack" error in recursive PL/pgSQL functions
description:
I'm encountering an internal error in PL/pgSQL functions that stems from `plpgsql_check`, which Supabase appears to load automatically via shared_preload_libraries. It's highly unexpected that correct SQL code can fail due to a library injected at the platform level that the user has no control over. Users shouldn't need to debug Supabase's infrastructure choices to run standard PostgreSQL functions.
The error occurs when running [pgstac](https://github.com/stac-utils/pgstac) during recursive PL/pgSQL function calls:


```sql
psycopg.errors.InternalError_: cannot find parent statement on pldbgapi2 call stack
CONTEXT:  PL/pgSQL function run_or_queue(text) line 13 at RETURN
SQL statement "SELECT run_or_queue(format($q$
            UPDATE collections
            SET content = jsonb_set_lax(
                content,
                '{extent}'::text[],
                collection_extent(%L, FALSE),
                true,
                'use_json_null'
            ) WHERE id=%L
            ;
        $q$, collection, collection))"
PL/pgSQL function update_partition_stats(text,boolean) line 57 at PERFORM
SQL statement "SELECT update_partition_stats('_items_1', 'f');"
PL/pgSQL function run_or_queue(text) line 11 at EXECUTE
SQL statement "SELECT run_or_queue(
        format('SELECT update_partition_stats(%L, %L);', _partition, istrigger)
    )"
PL/pgSQL function update_partition_stats_q(text,boolean) line 4 at PERFORM
```

## Investigation:

I have not installed any debugger extension:
* `SELECT * FROM pg_extension` shows no debugger-related extensions
* `SELECT proname FROM pg_proc WHERE proname LIKE '%pldbg%'` returns no rows
* `SELECT * FROM pg_available_extensions WHERE name LIKE '%debug%'` returns no rows

However, `SHOW shared_preload_libraries` returns:
```
pg_stat_statements, pgaudit, plpgsql, plpgsql_check, pg_cron, pg_net, pgsodium, timescaledb, auto_explain, pg_tle, plan_filter, supabase_vault
```

The error references `pldbgapi2`, which is part of the PL/pgSQL debugger infrastructure. I suspect `plpgsql_check` in the shared preload libraries is either compiled with debugger support or uses debugger-related call stack tracking that fails with nested recursive function calls.

## Possible Workaround?

Currently evaluating whether setting `plpgsql_check.mode = 'disabled'` on the affected functions will fix these errors.

```sql
ALTER FUNCTION pgstac.run_or_queue(text) SET plpgsql_check.mode = 'disabled';
ALTER FUNCTION pgstac.update_partition_stats(text, boolean) SET plpgsql_check.mode = 'disabled';
ALTER FUNCTION pgstac.update_partition_stats_q(text, boolean) SET plpgsql_check.mode = 'disabled';
```

title: Auth rate limit for sign-ups and sign-ins is not enforced despite configuration
description:
# Bug report

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application.
- [x] I confirm I have searched the Docs, GitHub Discussions, and Discord.

## Describe the bug

The **“Rate limit for sign-ups and sign-ins”** setting configured via the Supabase Dashboard or `config.toml` does not appear to be enforced.

Even when setting a very low limit (e.g. 5 requests per 5 minutes per IP), the Auth API allows significantly more sign-up/sign-in requests from the same IP before returning a rate limit error.

Eventually, requests are blocked, but only after approximately 30–50 requests, seemingly due to a different, more general rate limit.

## To Reproduce

1. Create a brand new Supabase project.
2. Go to **Dashboard → Authentication → Configuration → Rate limits**.
3. Set **Rate limit for sign-ups and sign-ins** to `5`.

   Alternatively, configure it in `config.toml`:
   ```toml
   # Number of sign up and sign-in requests that can be made in a 5 minute interval per IP address (excludes anonymous users).
   sign_in_sign_ups = 5
   ```

4. Use a simple script (e.g. Python) to repeatedly call the sign-up endpoint from the same IP address.
5. Observe that far more than 5 requests are accepted before any rate limit is triggered.

Eventually, requests fail with the following response:
```json
{
  "code": 429,
  "error_code": "over_request_rate_limit",
  "msg": "Request rate limit reached"
}
```

This error only occurs after ~30–50 requests, not after the configured limit of 5.

## Expected behavior

Once the configured limit (e.g. 5 requests per 5 minutes per IP) is reached, further sign-up/sign-in requests from the same IP should immediately be rejected with a rate limit error.

## Screenshots

N/A — the issue is reproducible without screenshots.

## System information

- OS: macOS
- Browser: N/A
- Version of supabase-js: N/A
- Version of Node.js: N/A

## Additional context

While investigating this issue, I noticed that this setting was previously mislabeled and actually updated `rate_limit_otp`. This appears to have been addressed in the following commit:

https://github.com/supabase/auth/commit/64ae65930be6ac3cac1709c4ed880629b5c09e36#diff-6164e6a3032e7fde89da82e26f3eeed2cd887f44abf42a2c25218cc5bf166e8e

However, even when using a project running the latest Supabase Auth version, the **sign-up/sign-in rate limit is still not enforced as configured**.

This suggests that either:
- the setting is still not wired correctly, or
- another rate limit is taking precedence while this one is effectively ignored.


I've also created this issue in the Auth repository, but feel free to close either in favor of the other.

title: Supabase Dashboard issues invalid ORDER BY syntax when querying pg_stat_statements (ORDER BY created_at:asc null)
description:
Body Hi Supabase team,

Summary

The Supabase Dashboard (application_name: "supabase/dashboard") issues an invalid SQL ORDER BY clause when attempting to fetch performance statistics, causing errors such as:
syntax error at or near ":" with fragment ORDER BY created_at:asc null
postgres log also shows: relation "pg_stat_statements" does not exist
This appears to be a dashboard bug in query generation/parsing. It does not break user applications but pollutes database logs and may confuse users.
Project context

Project ref: rbivamtmcadfnemqcvac
Timeframe: logs collected within the last 24 hours (I can provide precise timestamps below)
Observed behavior

The dashboard builds/executes an ORDER BY fragment using : instead of the SQL-standard space between column and direction and a proper NULLS clause. Example malformed fragment:
ORDER BY created_at:asc null
The dashboard also issues queries referencing pg_stat_statements even when the extension is not installed, producing relation "pg_stat_statements" does not exist errors.
Why this matters

Pollutes Postgres logs with syntax and missing-relation errors.
Should be handled gracefully by the dashboard (use valid syntax and check for pg_stat_statements presence before querying).
Relevant log excerpts (redacted where appropriate)

Postgres log showing missing extension error:

timestamp: 1771111306624000
severity: ERROR
message: relation "pg_stat_statements" does not exist
Postgres connection entries showing dashboard as source (examples):

timestamp: 1771111647827000 — connection authorized: user=postgres database=postgres application_name=supabase/dashboard SSL enabled ...
timestamp: 1771111538470000 — connection authorized: user=postgres database=postgres application_name=supabase/dashboard SSL enabled ...
timestamp: 1771111528356000 — connection authorized: user=postgres database=postgres application_name=supabase/dashboard SSL enabled ... (Multiple repeated connections with application_name = supabase/dashboard observed.)
API / access logs (examples of normal requests from dashboard / UI around same timeframe):

id: 946b67cc-8607-42fb-8c50-8e37826f722f
method: GET
path: /rest/v1/articles?select=...&order=created_at.desc
status_code: 200
timestamp: 1771111469757000
id: 98011ccd-00c5-4ac0-8763-1bce8ea8d9b2
method: GET
path: /rest/v1/comments?select=...&order=created_at.desc
status_code: 200
timestamp: 1771111465485000
id: 2d7bb60e-a294-49c8-93e9-4c7ed4a04176
method: GET
path: /rest/v1/comments?select=...&order=created_at.asc
status_code: 200
timestamp: 1771110394039000
Example postgres ERROR entry (full raw line):

{"id":"5ae7d08d-b2a6-427a-b52e-0882acf0e5c3","identifier":"rbivamtmcadfnemqcvac","timestamp":1771111306624000,"error_severity":"ERROR","event_message":"relation "pg_stat_statements" does not exist"}
Example postgres connection raw line (dashboard source):

{"id":"ecd5bd81-8893-4f5e-b115-9eb38953d489","identifier":"rbivamtmcadfnemqcvac","timestamp":1771111647827000,"error_severity":"LOG","event_message":"connection authorized: user=postgres database=postgres application_name=supabase/dashboard SSL enabled (protocol=TLSv1.3, cipher=TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384, bits=256)"}
Notes on redaction

I have redacted API keys present in the logs (some API entries already show apikey=REDACTED).
Example IP addresses observed: 182.10.130.220 — include or redact per your preference. I have left them visible above but can remove them if you prefer full redaction.
Steps to reproduce (approximate)

Open Supabase Dashboard for any project without the pg_stat_statements extension installed.
Navigate to the Dashboard's performance / query insights page (where query performance or pg_stat_statements would be used).
Check Postgres logs for syntax errors or missing-relation errors similar to the ones above.
Expected behavior

Dashboard should construct valid ORDER BY clauses (e.g., ORDER BY created_at ASC NULLS LAST) rather than created_at:asc null.
Dashboard should check for pg_stat_statements availability before querying or handle missing extension gracefully.
Suggested fixes

Validate/sanitize ORDER BY clause generation to follow SQL standards (space-separated tokens and correct NULL ordering).
Wrap pg_stat_statements usage with a presence check:
Example: SELECT 1 FROM pg_extension WHERE extname = 'pg_stat_statements';
Or catch the missing relation error and skip that monitoring query.
Add unit/integration tests around query-building code paths and dashboards running on clusters without pg_stat_statements.
Additional context & offer

I can provide more exact raw log lines with timestamps (fully redacted on request), or a timeline of dashboard connections vs the error timestamps if helpful.
I can also test any patch/PR you provide to confirm the fix in my project.
Thanks — happy to help debug or test.

Any other question let me know _Originally posted by @mcerejo in [#40109](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/issues/40109#issuecomment-3544293381)_

title: Supabase Bug Report: RLS Policy Function Returns NULL While Same Function Works via RPC - JWT Claims Not Available in RLS Context
description:
# Supabase Bug Report: RLS Policy Function Returns NULL While Same Function Works via RPC - JWT Claims Not Available in RLS Context

---

## 🎯 Executive Summary {#executive-summary}

**The Issue in Brief:**

A custom PostgreSQL function that successfully extracts user ID from JWT claims when called via RPC (`supabase.rpc()`) returns NULL when evaluated within Row Level Security (RLS) policy context, causing RLS policies to fail and blocking INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations.

**Key Facts:**
- ✅ Function works correctly via RPC - returns authenticated user ID
- ❌ Same function returns NULL in RLS policy context
- ❌ RLS policies fail, blocking legitimate database operations
- ✅ User is properly authenticated with valid JWT token
- ✅ All client-side checks pass (session, token, headers)
- ✅ RLS policies are correctly configured

**Root Cause:**
PostgREST does not make JWT claims available (`request.jwt.claim.*` settings) in RLS policy evaluation context, even though they are available in RPC function execution context. This creates an inconsistency where the same function behaves differently depending on execution context.

**Impact:**
- Blocks core functionality (user data operations)
- Affects all tables with RLS policies using JWT-based user identification
- No secure workaround available
- Prevents production deployment with proper RLS security

**Proposed Fix:**
PostgREST should set `request.jwt.claim.*` settings before RLS policy evaluation, ensuring functions called from RLS policies can access JWT claims, just like they can in RPC context.
